text:
Agile is a project management approach, often used in software development. Instead of planning everything upfront, teams work in short cycles called sprints. Each sprint typically lasts one to four weeks and ends with a working product.

Every day, the team has a brief standup meeting to discuss progress and any problems. All tasks are kept in a backlog, a prioritized list of work. Each task is described as a user story, which explains a feature from the end-user’s perspective. For example, "As a user, I want to reset my password." This allows iterative development, where the product is improved gradually through repeated cycles, based on feedback.
